evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at qltysh-testing/httpx. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/httpx --head qlty-setup --base master
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Python project using ruff. Detects existing pyproject.toml with ruff config. Enables ruff, trufflehog, markdownlint, yamllint, actionlint. Asks user about modes and smells before committing.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/httpx
PR branch is qlty-setup
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
ruff plugin is enabled (Python project)
trufflehog plugin is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled (markdown files present)
actionlint is enabled (GitHub Actions workflows present)
Detected existing pyproject.toml with ruff configuration and referenced it via config_files or noted it is auto-detected
Did NOT enable flake8 or black (ruff covers both)
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream encode/httpx repo

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is at qltysh-testing/qlty-test-mono. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/qlty-test-mono --head qlty-setup --base main

This is a fullstack monorepo with three subdirectories: frontend/ (React + TypeScript, has .eslintrc.js and package.json with all @typescript-eslint and react eslint deps already listed in devDependencies), backend/ (Python FastAPI, has pyproject.toml with [tool.ruff] and [tool.mypy]), and infra/ (Terraform). Each subdir has its own language stack. Set up qlty.toml with appropriate prefix scoping. For the frontend eslint plugin, use package_file pointing to frontend/package.json with package_filters rather than duplicating extra_packages. Include tsc for TypeScript type checking (tsconfig.json present in frontend/).
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a TypeScript+Python+Terraform monorepo. Uses prefix scoping for each subdirectory. Frontend eslint uses package_file with package_filters. Separate ruff and mypy for backend. checkov and tflint for infra.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/qlty-test-mono
PR branch is qlty-setup
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
eslint plugin has prefix = "frontend" to scope it to the frontend subdirectory
eslint plugin uses package_file referencing frontend/package.json (not extra_packages) because devDependencies already has all needed packages
eslint plugin uses package_filters to limit which packages are installed
ruff plugin is scoped to the backend/ subdirectory via prefix or [[exclude]]
tflint or checkov is scoped to the infra/ subdirectory
trufflehog is enabled globally (not scoped)
tsc is included for TypeScript type checking
Security plugins (trufflehog, osv-scanner or trivy) are set to block mode

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is at qltysh-testing/qlty-test-data-science. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/qlty-test-data-science --head qlty-setup --base main

This is a Python data science repo. The code has functions with 6-8 parameters (train_model, preprocess_features, evaluate_model) which is normal for ML pipelines, and has three structurally identical summarize_*_data functions (train/test/validation) which is an intentional pattern for separate dataset stats. Set up qlty.toml with smells configuration appropriate for data science code: raise function_parameters threshold, adjust function_complexity, and handle the intentional duplication.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Python data science project. Smells section includes raised thresholds appropriate for ML code. Uses language.python.smells overrides or global threshold adjustments. Either disables identical_code or raises duplication threshold.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/qlty-test-data-science
PR branch is qlty-setup
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
ruff is enabled (Python project with pyproject.toml [tool.ruff])
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
smells section is present
function_parameters threshold is raised above the default of 4 (either via [language.python.smells] or [smells.function_parameters]) to accommodate ML functions with many params
Either smells.identical_code.enabled = false OR smells.duplication.nodes_threshold is raised, to account for the intentionally identical summarize_train/test/validation_data functions
Did NOT enable both ruff and flake8

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at qltysh-testing/puma. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/puma --head qlty-setup-v2 --base main

This is the Puma Ruby web server. It has a Gemfile that already declares rubocop and rubocop-performance gems. It also has .rubocop.yml and .rubocop_todo.yml. Configure qlty.toml to use package_file pointing to the Gemfile (with package_filters) rather than extra_packages, since rubocop extensions are already declared there. Reference both .rubocop.yml and .rubocop_todo.yml in config_files. Add [[triage]] rules to downgrade known-noisy auto-correctable rubocop Style offenses to monitor mode.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Ruby project. Rubocop plugin uses package_file = "Gemfile" with package_filters instead of extra_packages. Both .rubocop.yml and .rubocop_todo.yml referenced in config_files. [[triage]] block downgrade noisy rubocop style rules.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/puma
PR branch is qlty-setup-v2
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
rubocop plugin is enabled
rubocop plugin uses package_file = "Gemfile" (not extra_packages) since rubocop and rubocop-performance are already in the Gemfile
rubocop plugin uses package_filters to limit which gems are installed
rubocop plugin has config_files referencing .rubocop.yml
At least one [[triage]] block is present to handle noisy rubocop rules (e.g., Style/* rules set to monitor)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled (markdown files present)
Did NOT use extra_packages for rubocop extensions since Gemfile already declares them

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at qltysh-testing/spring-petclinic. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/spring-petclinic --head qlty-setup-v2 --base main

This is a Java Spring Boot application. It uses checkstyle (via Maven plugin in pom.xml). It also has docker-compose.yml and k8s/ directory with Kubernetes manifests. Set up qlty.toml with: google-java-format and pmd for Java code quality; checkov for the Docker and K8s files; kube-linter for the k8s/ manifests; smells with per-language Java overrides (Java enterprise code typically needs higher complexity thresholds); and [[triage]] rules to handle known-noisy pmd findings.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Java Spring Boot project. Includes google-java-format, pmd, checkov for Docker/K8s, kube-linter. Java smells thresholds adjusted. [[triage]] present for noisy pmd rules.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/spring-petclinic
PR branch is qlty-setup-v2
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
google-java-format is enabled for Java formatting
pmd is enabled for Java static analysis
checkov is enabled (docker-compose.yml and k8s manifests present) and set to block mode
kube-linter is enabled (k8s/ directory with Kubernetes manifests present)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
smells section is present with Java-appropriate threshold adjustments (function_complexity > 15 or language.java.smells override)
At least one [[triage]] block is present (e.g., to downgrade noisy pmd rules to monitor)
yamllint is enabled (YAML files present including docker-compose.yml and k8s manifests)

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at qltysh-testing/terraform-aws-vpc. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/terraform-aws-vpc --head qlty-setup-v2 --base master

This is a Terraform module for AWS VPC. It has GitHub Actions workflows. It uses tflint in its pre-commit config. Set up qlty.toml with: terraform for formatting; tflint for linting; trivy with both config and vuln drivers (IaC scanning + vulnerability scanning); checkov for IaC security, with affects_cache to ensure re-analysis when the Terraform files change; [[triage]] to handle known AWS-specific IaC findings that are intentional patterns in a reusable module; and actionlint + yamllint for the GitHub Actions workflows.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Terraform module. trivy uses drivers = ["config", "vuln"]. checkov has affects_cache. [[triage]] handles AWS module patterns. actionlint and yamllint enabled.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/terraform-aws-vpc
PR branch is qlty-setup-v2
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
terraform plugin is enabled for formatting
tflint plugin is enabled
trivy plugin is enabled with drivers that include "config" for IaC scanning
trivy drivers list does NOT include "fs-vuln" (invalid value)
checkov plugin is enabled and set to block mode
checkov plugin has affects_cache listing relevant Terraform config files
At least one [[triage]] block is present for IaC findings
actionlint is enabled (GitHub Actions workflows present)
yamllint is enabled (YAML files present)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This is a dedicated test repo at qltysh-testing/qlty-test-tsc. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/qlty-test-tsc --head qlty-setup --base main. Note: do NOT enable the tsc plugin — tsc causes build errors in Qlty Cloud on most repos. Instead demonstrate skip_upstream conceptually and focus on affects_cache + .qlty/configs/ storage.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml using affects_cache=["tsconfig.json"] on eslint, config stored in .qlty/configs/.eslintrc.js, skip_upstream documented in comments. No tsc plugin.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/qlty-test-tsc with branch qlty-setup
eslint plugin has affects_cache = ["tsconfig.json"]
Config file stored in .qlty/configs/ and referenced via config_files
Uses extra_packages with @typescript-eslint (v5 or v6 only, not v7+)
Does NOT include tsc plugin (known to cause build errors in Qlty Cloud)
Security plugins set to block mode

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at qltysh-testing/Alamofire. Default branch is master (not main). When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/Alamofire --head qlty-setup-v2 --base master
evals.expected_output: PR created with qlty.toml using version pin on swiftlint, [[triage]] with set.level='warning' downgrading a noisy swiftlint rule, and [[exclude]] block for generated/test files scoped to swiftlint only.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/Alamofire with branch qlty-setup-v2
swiftlint plugin has version = "..." pin
At least one [[triage]] block with set.level = "warning"
At least one [[exclude]] block scoped to a specific plugin
Security plugins set to block mode

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at qltysh-testing/pest. Default branch is 4.x (not main). When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/pest --head qlty-setup-v2 --base 4.x. Note: pest uses Laravel Pint for formatting (not php-cs-fixer/php-codesniffer) — skip PHP formatter plugin.
evals.expected_output: PR created with qlty.toml using package_file='composer.json' with package_filters for phpstan extensions, [[exclude]] block scoped to phpstan for test fixture directories.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/pest with branch qlty-setup-v2
phpstan plugin uses package_file or extra_packages for its composer extensions
[[exclude]] block scoped to phpstan, file_patterns excluding tests/Fixtures
No php-cs-fixer or php-codesniffer (project uses Laravel Pint)
Security plugins set to block mode

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at qltysh-testing/openapi-backend. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/openapi-backend --head qlty-setup-v2 --base main. Build on the existing v1 qlty-setup config by adding triage and exclude blocks.
evals.expected_output: PR created with qlty.toml adding [[triage]] with set.category='security' on spectral rule, [[exclude]] for generated types from tsc/eslint, [[triage]] with set.ignored=true in test files. Uses @typescript-eslint@5.62.0 (stable, same as v1).
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/openapi-backend with branch qlty-setup-v2
tsc plugin has skip_upstream = true
At least one [[triage]] block with set.category
[[exclude]] block for generated TypeScript types scoped to tsc and/or eslint
At least one [[triage]] block with set.ignored = true and file_patterns
Uses @typescript-eslint@5.62.0 (matching v1 which was confirmed working)

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a test repo at qltysh-testing/qlty-test-smells. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo qltysh-testing/qlty-test-smells --head qlty-setup --base main. The repo has intentional duplication: 3 nearly-identical Ruby methods and 3 identical Python functions, each with 7 parameters.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml using smells.duplication.nodes_threshold=12, smells.duplication.filter_patterns for test exclusion, language.ruby.smells.function_parameters.threshold=6, and [[triage]] ignoring Metrics/ParameterLists in test files.
expectations: Created a PR on qltysh-testing/qlty-test-smells with branch qlty-setup
[smells.duplication] nodes_threshold set below 25 to catch intentional duplicates
[smells.duplication] filter_patterns excludes test directories
[language.ruby.smells] function_parameters.threshold set to catch 7-param methods
[[triage]] block ignores rubocop Metrics/ParameterLists in test files
Uses [[plugin]] blocks (not [plugins.enabled_plugins.X] format)
